Dear Mr. Ghatak,

Thank you for your email of the 24th of October.  I am sorry that I 
have not been able to respond to you sooner.  I am currently in the process 
of moving to London and will be unable to respond to your email.  I hope 
you will be able to get in touch with me soon.

I am currently working on a project for Enron in India and would be very happy to 
discuss the project with you.  I am currently in London and will be in New York 
on the 15th of November.  Please let me know if you are available to meet with me.

I look forward to hearing from you.

Kind regards,

Sara Shackleton
Enron Wholesale Services

The Alzheimer's Association is pleased to announce the launch of the new 
Alzheimer's Microbial Data Center. The Center will provide a central location for 
organization and sharing of data on the causes and treatments of Alzheimer's disease. 
The Center will be a resource for researchers, health professionals, and the public 
to better understand the disease and develop new treatments.

The Center will be a collaborative effort between the Alzheimer's Association, the University of 
Texas Health Science Center at Houston, and the Center for Infectious Disease Research and 
Control at the University of Texas Health Science Center at Houston.

India: Enron to sell Dabhol power project

05/28/2001
Reuters English News Service
(C) Reuters Limited 2001.

NEW DELHI, May 28 (Reuters) - U.S. energy major Enron Corp. will sell its stake in the $2.9 billion Dabhol Power Co. project in India, the company said on Monday. 
The company will sell its 50 percent stake in the project to the Maharashtra State Electricity Board (MSEB) for $1.5 billion, Enron said in a statement. 
The sale will help MSEB repay $1.1 billion in loans it has extended to the project, Enron said. 
The sale of the stake will help Enron reduce debt on the project, which is the largest foreign investment in India. 
The Dabhol power project, located in the western Indian state of Maharashtra, is India's largest foreign direct investment. 
The project has been mired in a dispute between Enron and MSEB, which has refused to pay Enron for power supplied to the project.

The purpose of this seminar is to provide an overview of the mental health 
issues facing the Enron employee community.  The seminar will be 
hosted by the Enron Employee Assistance Program (EAP) and will be held at 
the Enron Center South.  

The seminar will be held from 9:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m. on Thursday, February 
15, 2001.  

The cost for this seminar is $50.  

If you have any questions, please call the EAP office at ext. 3-6992.

Thank you,

Enron Employee Assistance Program

(See attached file: Enron Employee Assistance Program - February 2001.doc)

I'm not sure what you mean by "right image."  I think that the
right image is the image that is most likely to be understood by
the average person.  If you want to be more specific, I think that the
right image is the image that is most likely to be understood by
the average person who is not a lawyer.  I think that the image that is most
likely to be understood by the average person who is a lawyer is the image

ship more easily, ensure consistent levels of service, and 
ensure that we are able to provide the best possible customer service to 
all of our customers.  

We are pleased to announce that we have appointed John Suttle as our new 
Chief Operating Officer.  John has been with Enron for over 15 years and 
has been responsible for the development of Enron's wholesale and 
retail businesses.  John will be responsible for the overall management of 
Enron's wholesale and retail businesses.  

John will report to me and will report directly to the Office of the Chairman.  

I would like to thank John for his many contributions to Enron and wish him the very 
best of success in his new role.  

I look forward to working with John and the rest of the Enron Wholesale Services organization 
in the months ahead.  

Sincerely,

Jeff SkillingMessage-ID: <14897476.1075858176531.JavaMail.evans@thyme>
